MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
LENORE C. DEFIESTA FROM WISON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP, 433-0990, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DANIEL BERKO

No other appearances on this matter. MS. DEFIESTA, IN BEHALF OF COUNSEL DANIEL BERKO, REQUESTED FOR A TELEPHONIC HEARING AND CONTINUANCE ON THIS MATTER DUE TO MR. BERKO BEING IN THE HOSPITAL FOR EMERGENCY REASONS. THE COURT DENIED THE REQUEST FOR A TELEPHONIC HEARING AND The Court MODIFIED it's tentative ruling as follows: THE COURT CONTINUED DANIEL BERKO'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF LARRY DUBAN TO JANUARY 19, 2007. (301/PJB/ck) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Lisa Schwam, CSR #8994

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-16-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
LENORE C. DEFIESTA FROM WISON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP, 433-0990, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT AND RESPONDING PARTY

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted it's tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF LARRY DUBAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Moving papers do not comply with C R C 3.1350 (d) or (f). Issues tendered do not fully resolve a cause of action or duty within the meaning of C C P 437 c (f) (1). Wrongful termination claim in complaint is premised on the landlord's dominant motive not being recovery of possession rather than on late payment of initial relocation installment. There is a triable issue of material fact as to the affect of the settlement agreement on the tenant's rights to payment. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/CK) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Lisa Schwam, CSR #8994
